name: compound-interpretive-value
description: A compound interpretive value framework: one question decomposes into ordered, individually-interpretive sub-claims (value synthesis, output-vs-irreplaceability driver decomposition, big-game-vs-average context conditioning) over a shared, deliberately rich subjects map. Each sub-claim is adjudicated under a declared weighting lens; the judge composes the sub-verdicts into the six on-chain fields. The attack surface is interpretive composition: lens application, cross-claim coherence, confidence composition, and the contestable tier/lens classification of an inflated evidence set (progression_metrics, percentile_ranks, big_game_splits, tactical_role_profile). v1.0 is an honest first attempt; addendums are added as rounds reveal real cracks.

sub_verdicts.description: Eval-only. Per-claim sub-verdict records. On-chain parser ignores.

composition_audit.description: Eval-only. The judge's explicit composition arithmetic.

cross_claim_consistency.description: Eval-only. 'ok' or a description of the violated crossClaimRef.
